text.title: AI Incident Triage Matrix Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every dimension in the matrix has anchors written for the 1, 3, and 5 levels — minimum. No anchors → no scoring.
rule.rationale: Unanchored scales drift between raters. '4 out of 5' means nothing without a behavioral anchor.

rule.statement: Each score must cite the specific evidence used (quote, log line, trace id, dashboard URL). A score without evidence is a guess.
rule.rationale: Evidence forces the rater to ground the score. Unevidenced scores fail audit and inter-rater reliability.

rule.statement: For decisions ≥$10k or strategic, require ≥2 independent raters and reconciliation if scores diverge by >1 point on any dimension.
rule.rationale: Single-rater scores are dominated by mood, recency, and halo. Reconciliation forces explicit talk-through.

rule.statement: Final composite is a weighted blend with weights fixed BEFORE scoring. Post-hoc weight tuning is rubric-laundering.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc weight tuning matches the desired outcome. Pre-registration of weights protects against motivated reasoning.

rule.statement: Output MUST assign the incident to exactly one of the 5 lanes (model-regression / prompt-injection / data-drift / upstream-API / cost-runaway). 'Other' is forbidden — force-fit until evidence supports a lane.
rule.rationale: 'Other' lanes route to no mitigation owner. Forcing classification surfaces the strongest signal and routes to the right team.

rule.statement: Skip when n<3 instances OR single-criterion decision OR no AI-specific surface OR raters untrained.
rule.rationale: Below thresholds the overhead exceeds value; mis-scoped raters introduce noise that exceeds the signal.
